impact: tools/validate.sh --target misroutes all three ecosystem fixture families (preflight_verdict, ecosystem_baseline, engineering_finding), so a targeted invocation against any of these fixtures gives zero discriminating signal -- a malformed ecosystem artifact would not be caught by a --target invocation, only by the full sweep (C12).
closure_criteria: check_c11_or_c4_target's content routing recognizes the three ecosystem schema families (e.g. filename-prefix or dual-key heuristics) with matching negative fixtures, per docs/planning/DEBT.md NDEBT-015's own remediation note; candidate scope for the next self-compliance feature, not this one.

impact: Historically, schema/qa_verdict.schema.json required keys the reference implementation's own .agent/qa/*.json verdicts did not carry, and nothing validated .agent/qa/*.json against a schema at all.
closure_criteria: The shipped schemas are reconciled to the produced artifact shapes, and validate.sh gains a check enforcing them against every .agent/qa/*.json, .agent/contracts/*.json, and .agent/run_state.json artifact.

impact: C12's fixture-polarity classification regex has confirmed dodges for FUTURE fixture filenames (full-word negative_, uppercase _NEG_, false-trigger _invalid_handling-style names) -- none of the 15 currently shipped fixtures are affected, but a future fixture family could silently defeat the polarity check.
closure_criteria: Either normalize the fixture-naming convention in a fixtures README (authoritative token list) or harden the regex (case-insensitive + full-token match) with fixture-name conformance asserted by C12 itself, per docs/planning/DEBT.md NDEBT-016's own remediation note.

impact: tools/ecosystem_preflight.py's ci_references field is repo-HEAD-anchored with an honest KNOWN LIMITATION note (no real CI-run lookup); this second dogfood run's own baseline again carries the same honest limitation, now exercised for a second real execution.
closure_criteria: Wire a real CI-run lookup (e.g. a GitHub Actions API call keyed off the repository/commit) into build_baseline_document's ci_references synthesis, per docs/planning/DEBT.md NDEBT-017's own remediation note; candidate scope for the next ecosystem-tooling feature, not this one.

impact: This feature's own second dogfood preflight run again surfaced its own in-flight contract/qa-review artifacts and evidence-output directory as untracked exceptions inside the very run they help produce -- the same self-reference friction class NDEBT-018 already catalogues, now reproduced a second time for real.
closure_criteria: Per docs/planning/DEBT.md NDEBT-018's own remediation notes: (1) consider a clean-worktree/scratch-checkout isolation mode for the preflight tool's self-fixture invocation; (2) codify durably that any orchestrator-owned tracked-state write relevant to a gate decision must be committed before the corresponding gated CLI invocation runs. Candidate scope beyond this feature.
